programs.name: Wyoming Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Wyoming Medicaid is a state and federally funded program that covers the cost of care for low-income or medically needy seniors, including long-term care services like nursing home care when functional needs are met. It helps pay for healthcare, prescriptions, and related services to support seniors' health needs.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ or medically needy individuals who meet income, resource, and functional eligibility criteria.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers nursing home care and long-term services with state funding of $53M+ for related waivers (SFY data).[1][3]

programs.name: Community Choices Waiver (CCW)
programs.what_it_does: The Community Choices Waiver is a Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) waiver program that provides long-term care services in home and community settings as an alternative to institutional care. It supports seniors and disabled adults to live independently with services like personal care and homemaker support.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Ages 65+ or ages 19-64 with disabilities requiring nursing home level of care.[1]

programs.name: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: No Wyoming-specific PACE programs identified in available sources. PACE generally provides comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings, including adult day health care and transportation.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home eligible seniors 55+ who can live safely in the community.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing for qualifying low-income beneficiaries. Seniors apply through the Wyoming Department of Health, which covers Part A/B premiums and other costs based on eligibility tier (Qualified Medicare Beneficiary, Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary, Qualifying Individual).[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries meeting specific income and resource limits.
programs.benefit_value: Full/partial coverage of Medicare premiums and costs; varies by program.[3]

programs.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly food assistance benefits to buy groceries for low-income households. Wyoming offers senior-specific deductions for medical expenses over $35/month and simplified applications for those 60+.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ with household income up to $1,580/month (individual).

programs.name: Wyoming Energy Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides financial assistance to help with heating costs during winter months. Benefits are issued October through March to eligible low-income households.[2]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and sealing to reduce energy costs and improve comfort. Available to low-income households including seniors.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free weatherization services (value varies by home).[2]

programs.name: Wyoming State Health Insurance Information Program (WSHIIP/SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free, confidential counseling to Medicare beneficiaries on rights, options, supplements, and problem resolution. Trained volunteers assist with Medicare, Medicaid, and Part D questions.[5]

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (via Title III-B Support Services)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Provided through senior centers funded by state and federal programs.[1][6]
programs.benefit_value: Suggested donation; funded by state/federal sources.[1][6]

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers support services like respite care, training, and counseling to family caregivers. Helps caregivers maintain their health while assisting older relatives.[7]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid training and employment in community service roles to unemployed low-income seniors. Aims to help transition to unsubsidized employment.[1]

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors (via Wyoming Senior Services Board)
programs.what_it_does: Provides legal aid to address issues like abuse, exploitation, and access to benefits for vulnerable older adults. Funded through state appropriations to senior centers.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Legal services funding via state board.[1]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, resolving complaints and protecting rights. Operates statewide to ensure quality care.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes and assisted living in WY.

programs.name: Wyoming Home Services Program
programs.what_it_does: The Wyoming Home Services (WyHS) program provides services to qualified individuals at risk of premature institutionalization. These services are designed to keep people in the least restrictive environment for as long as possible.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Qualified individuals at risk of premature institutionalization, typically seniors, served in Albany, Fremont, and Park Counties.[2]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific program administered by Wyoming Senior Citizens, Inc. (WSCI) to prevent institutionalization through home-based support, distinct from standard federal OAA or Medicaid HCBS.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Home services to maintain independence; specific monetary value not detailed in sources.

programs.name: Wyoming Senior Companion Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides assistance and friendship to older adults who have difficulty with daily living tasks such as shopping or paying bills. AmeriCorps Seniors volunteers help keep seniors independent longer and provide respite to family caregivers.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Older adults needing help with daily tasks and their family caregivers; services offered across Wyoming.[2]
programs.whats_unique: Statewide implementation through Wyoming Senior Citizens, Inc. emphasizing companionship and independence in a rural state context, building on federal AmeriCorps Seniors but locally focused.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Non-monetary companionship and task assistance; volunteers report health benefits.
